caveats: Funnel evidence is descriptive and based on synthetic sample size.
title: Recommendation interaction is the only observed completed funnel

caveats: Retention rates use observed denominators and synthetic cohorts.
title: Most observed retention cohorts returned, with one signup cohort at zero return

caveats: Churn predictions are risk indicators, not certainties.
title: Automation activity is the strongest churn model feature in the evidence

caveats: Segment labels are analytical interpretations, not causal identities.
title: The largest interpretable segment is inactive or declining users

caveats: Recommendation scores are rankings, not probabilities.
title: Segment-aware popularity is the selected recommendation baseline

caveats: Experiment decisions include sample-size and guardrail caveats.
title: Experiment evidence blocks two treatments and leaves two without clear evidence

caveats: Cross-domain insight combines evidence but does not prove causality.
title: Activation, retention and experiment guardrails point to setup quality as a priority

caveats: All findings are derived from synthetic NexaFlow evidence.
title: Synthetic evidence requires cautious interpretation
